LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, JR., ROBERT HALLIGAN AND DATA TRANSFER, LLC DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUDULENT TRANSFER.) DATA TRANSFER IS THE "DEBTOR" AND RECEIVED THE ASSETS. DATA TRANSFER NEVER "TRANSFERRED" ANY ASSETS WITHIN THE MEANING OF CC SECTION 3439.01(I). DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. REGARDING UNJUST ENRICHMENT CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A BASIS TO VOID THE CONTRACT AND SEEK RESTITUTION. REGARDING THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFICALLY HIGHLIGHT THE UNLAWFUL CONDUCT AND IDENTIFY THE LEGISLATIVE POLICY THAT SUPPORTS THE CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY FACTS THAT SHOW THESE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE SEPARATION AGREEMENT. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, JR., ROBERT HALLIGAN, AND, DATA TRANSFER, LLC DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. COUNSEL FAILED TO GIVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART, AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF MUST CLEARLY PLEAD THAT THE ACTIONS THAT FORM THE BASIS OF THESE CLAIMS OCCURRED AFTER THE EXECUTION OF THE SEPARATION AGREEMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, JR., ROBERT HALLIGAN, and, DATA TRANSFER, LLC DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (fraudulent transfer.) Data Transfer is the "debtor" and received the assets. Data Transfer never "transferred" any assets within the meaning of CC section 3439.01(i). Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 3rd and 4th causes of action. Regarding unjust enrichment cause of action, plaintiff must plead a basis to void the contract and seek restitution. Regarding the UCL cause of action, plaintiff must specifically highlight the unlawful conduct and identify the legislative policy that supports the claim for unfair business practice. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, JR., ROBERT HALLIGAN, and, DATA TRANSFER, LLC DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Robert H. Bohn, Sr., Esq. of Bohn & Bohn LLP (408) 279-4222, Appearing thru CourtCall Plaintiff and opposing party. Counsel failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to first cause of action for breach of contract and covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action for fraud and third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff must clearly plead that the actions that form the basis of these claims occurred after the execution of the separation agreement. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Apr-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DATA TRANSFER OF NEVADA, LLC, KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, JR., and, ROBERT HALLIGAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Robert H. Bohn, Esq. of Bohn & Bohn LLP (408) 279-4222, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; John G. Kerkorian, Esq. of Ballard Spahr Andrews & Ingersoll (602) 798-5408, Appearing thru CourtCall, appearing for Defendants and moving party; Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second, and third causes of action. Plaintiff must plead with greater specificity facts that show these claims are not barred by the separation agreement. Demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action. (302CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
